nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated; found on coral reef slopes prone to moderate currents, typically near large coral heads. Adults tend to occupy depths greater than 20 m; juveniles have been found in shallow lagoons and protected bays.

diet.notes: Tends to accept meaty foods such as krill, shrimp, crab, and whole silversides. Has been reported to take frozen preparations over time. Small live fish may be taken as prey. Large water volume and robust filtration are generally advised given the species' heavy bioload.

summary: A large Indo-Pacific soapfish (to ~35 cm) with striking spotted patterning, generally considered expert-level due to its predatory nature and a toxic skin secretion that can prove lethal to tank inhabitants if the fish is severely stressed or dies.
careNotes:
The Leaflip Grouper is a large, ambush-oriented predator that has been reported to grow to approximately 35 cm in well-kept aquariums. Tanks of at least 1200 L are generally advised to provide adequate territory and dilute bioload — note that LiveAquaria lists a minimum of 125 gallons (~473 L), while dedicated aquarium encyclopedias (ReefApp, Fishy.wiki) consistently recommend 1200 L; the larger figure is preferred here given the species' adult size and activity needs.

The species produces a toxic skin mucus (grammistin) that deters predators in the wild. In captivity this toxin has been known to be released in quantity when the fish is severely stressed or dying, and multiple reports indicate it can be lethal to all other inhabitants in the aquarium. A tight-fitting lid and stable water conditions are strongly recommended to minimize stress.

Juveniles tend to be more skittish; adults generally become tamer over time. The species is best kept singly in a predator tank with large, robust tankmates of similar size. Small fish, shrimp, and crabs are typically treated as prey.

Water-temperature data across sources shows some spread: LiveAquaria and Fishy.wiki suggest 22–26°C while FishBase records a wild range of 21–29°C; the filed range of 22–27°C represents an average across these references.

The minimum tank volume disagreement (473 L vs 1200 L) exceeds a 2× spread; dataStatus is set to needs_review so a human reviewer can confirm the appropriate recommendation.

Groupers in general are protogynous hermaphrodites (female-first sex changers), and Pogonoperca punctata is believed to follow this pattern, though specific documentation for this species in captivity is limited.

Also sold under the trade names Clown Grouper, Spotted Soapfish, Snowflake Soapfish, and Leaflip Soapfish.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Product page for Leaflip Grouper. Lists 125 gal minimum tank size, 72-78°F, SG 1.020-1.025, pH 8.1-8.4, Expert Only care level. Care level and water parameters used as primary reference.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Scientific data: max size 35.0 cm TL, wild temperature range 21.2-29°C, family Grammistidae, order Perciformes. Native range confirmed: Comoros to Line/Marquesan/Society Islands, north to Japan, south to New Caledonia, and South Africa. Used for taxonomy, size, and temperature verification.

additional.name: ReefApp Encyclopedia
additional.notes: Lists 1200 L minimum tank, temperature ~26°C, SG ~1.026, reef safe with caution. Tank size and salinity upper bound cross-checked here. Significantly larger tank recommendation than LiveAquaria (>2× spread); contributes to needs_review status.

additional.name: Fishy.wiki
additional.notes: Lists 1200 L minimum tank, temperature 22.2-25.6°C, SG 1.020-1.025, pH 8.1-8.4, kH 8-12, care level Advanced. Confirms large tank requirement; kH range sourced here. Notes escape risk and toxic secretion concern.
